questions.explanation: When adding two negative integers, we add their absolute values and put a negative sign.

questions.text: The product of two negative integers is always:
questions.explanation: According to the rules of multiplication, the product of two negative integers is a positive integer.

questions.text: What is the additive identity for integers?
questions.explanation: Zero is called the additive identity because adding zero to any integer results in the same integer.

questions.text: Which property is shown by: a × (b + c) = a × b + a × c?
questions.explanation: This is the distributive property of multiplication over addition.
If a negative integer is multiplied an odd number of times, the product is negative.
When a negative integer is divided by a positive integer, the quotient is negative.
Subtracting a negative number is the same as adding its positive counterpart: 10 + 5 = 15.
-1 is the largest negative integer because it is closest to zero on the number line.

questions.text: What is the multiplicative identity for integers?
questions.explanation: 1 is the multiplicative identity because any integer multiplied by 1 remains unchanged.

questions.text: If the sum of two integers is -10 and one of them is 2, the other is:
questions.explanation: Let the other be x. x + 2 = -10 => x = -10 - 2 = -12.
